title: heliopsis helianthoides var. occidentalis
intro: Heliopsis helianthoides var. occidentalis, also known as Western Oxeye Sunflower, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers with dark centers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Western Oxeye Sunflower include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory congestion. Western Oxeye Sunflower can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can be used to make a medicinal extract, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
